reply:
[[BLOCK:slide34_shape1]]
The Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) (贝叶斯信息准则 (Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC))) imposes a stricter penalty.  
However, Akaike’s Information Criterion (AIC) (赤池信息准则 (Akaike’s Information Criterion (AIC))) and AICc outperform BIC.  
AIC and AICc are based on the principle of information gain.  
Bayesian methods require prior input, which is often a subject of debate.  
AIC is asymptotically optimal for model selection based on the criterion of minimum mean squared error, whereas BIC is not asymptotically optimal (Burnham & Anderson, 2004; Yang, 2005).
